meta.name: Classic Risotto (Parmigiano + Butter)
meta.subtitle: Creamy stovetop rice made by gradual stock absorption; base recipe for many variations

source.name: ssa-core
notes: Canonical risotto base (risotto bianco) suitable for branching variations (mushroom, seafood, saffron, etc.).
Designed for deterministic planning: clear roles, timing, and equipment requirements.

yield.notes: Serves 4 as a main, 6 as a primi (first course).

notes: Best eaten immediately for classic creamy texture.
Leftovers can be repurposed into arancini or baked risotto cakes.
Primary flavor is buttery, nutty, and cheesy; balance salt carefully because stock and cheese both add salt.

ingredients.label: Stock (vegetable or chicken), kept hot
notes: About 5 cups; you may not use all of it.
Keep at a bare simmer to maintain risotto temperature.

ingredients.label: Unsalted butter, divided
ingredients.notes: Half for cooking, half for finishing (mantecatura).

ingredients.label: Olive oil
ingredients.notes: About 1 tablespoon; optional but helps prevent butter scorching.

ingredients.label: Shallot (or onion), finely minced
ingredients.notes: About 1 large shallot or 1/2 small onion.

ingredients.label: Arborio rice (or Carnaroli)
ingredients.notes: About 1 3/4 cups. Carnaroli is excellent if available.

ingredients.label: Dry white wine (optional)
ingredients.notes: About 1/2 cup. If omitted, use an extra ladle of stock with a squeeze of lemon at the end (optional).

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
ingredients.notes: Start light; adjust at the end (stock + cheese add salt).

ingredients.label: Lemon zest (optional)
ingredients.notes: Nice brightness, especially if skipping wine.

ingredients.label: Lemon juice (optional)
ingredients.notes: A small squeeze at the end if needed for balance.

steps.title: Heat stock and prep aromatics
instructions: Warm stock in a saucepan and keep it at a bare simmer.
checks: Stock is hot (steaming) so it doesn’t cool the pan each addition.

steps.title: Sweat aromatics
instructions: In a wide saucepan, heat olive oil and 30 g butter over medium heat.
Add minced shallot/onion with a pinch of salt and cook 3–5 minutes until soft and translucent (not browned).
checks: Aromatics are soft and sweet, not browned.

steps.title: Toast rice (tostatura)
instructions: Add rice to the pan and stir to coat in fat.
Cook 2–3 minutes, stirring, until the grains look slightly translucent on the edges and smell lightly nutty.
checks: Rice is coated and warm; avoid browning the aromatics.

steps.title: Add wine (optional) and reduce
instructions: Pour in white wine (if using) and stir.
Simmer until the wine is mostly absorbed/evaporated and the alcohol smell is gone.
checks: Pan is not swimming; wine is mostly absorbed.

steps.title: Cook risotto by adding hot stock gradually
instructions: Add 1 ladle of hot stock (about 120 ml) and stir until mostly absorbed.
Continue adding stock 1 ladle at a time, stirring frequently, keeping the risotto at a gentle simmer.
After about 18–22 minutes, begin tasting the rice: it should be tender with a slight bite (al dente).
Adjust with smaller additions near the end to hit a loose, creamy texture.
checks: Rice is tender but not mushy.
Texture is 'all’onda' (waves): loose enough to spread slightly, not stiff.

steps.title: Finish (mantecatura) with butter and Parmesan
instructions: Turn heat to low (or off if pan is very hot).
Stir in remaining 30 g butter until glossy and emulsified.
Stir in grated Parmesan until creamy.
Taste and adjust salt and pepper.
Optional: add lemon zest and/or a small squeeze of lemon juice for brightness.
checks: Risotto looks glossy and creamy, not greasy or clumpy.

steps.title: Serve immediately
instructions: Serve hot in warm bowls or plates.
Optional garnish: parsley and extra Parmesan.
steps.notes: Risotto tightens as it sits; serve right away for best texture.

notes: Keep stock hot to maintain safe cooking temperature and proper texture.
If using chicken stock, treat as perishable; cool leftovers promptly and refrigerate within 2 hours.
Refrigerate up to 3 days.
Reheat gently with a splash of stock or water and a knob of butter to loosen.
Not ideal for classic risotto texture, but can be frozen for arancini use.
Thaw in fridge and reheat with liquid; use as filling or cakes.
Stirring is important for creaminess (starch release) but doesn’t need constant frantic stirring—just frequent.
A wide pan helps evaporation and even cooking.
If stock is very salty, reduce added salt until the end.
Sauté mushrooms separately until browned; add near the end with a little of their juices.
Finish with thyme and a touch more Parmesan.
Bloom saffron in 2 tablespoons warm stock and add around minute 10 of cooking.
Traditionally paired with osso buco.
Use vegetable stock.
Replace butter with olive oil or vegan butter.
Replace Parmesan with nutritional yeast or a vegan Parmesan substitute.
Chill risotto until firm.
Form balls with a small cube of mozzarella in the center, bread, then fry or bake.
Great for meal-prep snacks.
Plan leftover conversion rather than expecting the same creamy texture on reheat.
